---
title: "figure_1tables_12_3_25"
output: html_document
date: "2025-12-04"
---

```{r}
# ===============================
# Load packages
# ===============================
library(tidyverse)
library(gtsummary)
library(gt)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
library(readxl)
#library(maftools)
library(data.table)
library(RColorBrewer)
#library(gtExtras)
library(scales)

# imports from external file
import <- new.env()
source("../src/data_imports.R", local = import)

dir.create("out/1b",showWarnings=FALSE,recursive=TRUE)

path = "../data/Supplementary\ Tables.xlsx"
df <- import$patients(path) %>%
  mutate(age_years = age_at_diagnosis / 365.25)

# ========================================================
# 1. BASELINE DEMOGRAPHIC TABLE (Figure 1A)
# ========================================================
table1 <- df %>%
  select(sex, race, age_years, cancer_type, cancer_subclass) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{median} ({p25}, {p75})",
                     all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"),
    missing = "no"
  ) %>%
  bold_labels() %>%
  as_gt()

# To save:
gtsave(table1, "out/1b/baseline_characteristics.html")
gtsave(table1, "out/1b/baseline_characteristics.pdf")

# ========================================================
# 2. AGE DISTRIBUTION (Figure 1B)
# ========================================================
p_age <- df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = sex, y = age_years, fill = sex)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.6, color = "black") +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, outlier.shape = NA, color = "black") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(title = "Age Distribution by Sex", x = "", y = "Age (years)")

# ========================================================
# 3. SEX DISTRIBUTION (Figure 1C)
# ========================================================
p_sex <- df %>%
  count(sex) %>%
  mutate(p = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = sex, y = p)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_text(aes(label = percent(p)), vjust = -0.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  labs(title = "Sex Distribution", x = "", y = "Percent")

# ========================================================
# 4. RACE / ETHNICITY DOT PLOT (Figure 1D)
# ========================================================
p_race <- df %>%
  count(race) %>%
  mutate(p = n / sum(n),
         race = fct_reorder(race, p)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = p, y = race)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, color = "firebrick") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  labs(title = "Race", x = "Percent", y = "")

# ========================================================
# 5. CANCER TYPE DOT PLOT (Figure 1E)
# ========================================================
p_cancer_type <- df %>%
  count(cancer_type) %>%
  mutate(p = n / sum(n),
         cancer_type = fct_reorder(cancer_type, p)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = p, y = cancer_type)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, color = "darkgreen") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  labs(title = "Cancer Type Distribution", x = "Percent", y = "")

# ========================================================
# 6. CANCER SUBCLASS HEATMAP (Figure 1F)
# ========================================================
# Filter out NA subclasses, and keep only cancer types that have >=1 non-NA subclass
subclass_df <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(cancer_subclass)) %>%        # remove NA subclass rows
  count(cancer_type, cancer_subclass) %>%    # compute subclass counts
  group_by(cancer_type) %>%
  mutate(total_type_n = sum(n)) %>%          # size of each cancer type AFTER removing NA
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(total_type_n > 0) %>%               # keep only cancer types with at least 1 subclass
  group_by(cancer_type) %>%
  mutate(p = n / sum(n))                     # percent within each cancer type

# choose how many subclasses to keep per cancer type
TOP_N <- 8

subclass_df_top <- subclass_df %>%
  arrange(cancer_type, desc(p)) %>%
  group_by(cancer_type) %>%
  slice_head(n = TOP_N)    # top N subclasses

p_subclass_facet_top <- subclass_df_top %>%
  mutate(cancer_subclass = fct_reorder(cancer_subclass, p)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = p, y = cancer_subclass)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "navy") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  facet_wrap(~ cancer_type, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Top Cancer Subclasses per Cancer Type",
    subtitle = paste("Top", TOP_N, "most common subclasses per cancer type"),
    x = "Percent of Patients Within Cancer Type",
    y = "Cancer Subclass"
  )


# ========================================================
# 7. CREATE MULTI-PANEL FIGURE FOR PDF
# ========================================================

combined_plot <- 
  (p_age | p_sex) /
  (p_race | p_cancer_type) /
  p_subclass_facet_top +
  plot_annotation(title = "Cohort Demographics Summary")


# Example save:
ggsave("out/1b/cohort_demographics.pdf", combined_plot, width = 12, height = 16)

```
#Create tables
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(gt)
library(scales)

#===========================================================
# Helper: create a gt table with counts + percent
#===========================================================
make_table <- function(df, var, title = NULL) {
  
  var_label <- if (!is.null(title)) title else rlang::as_label(enquo(var))
  
  df %>%
    count({{ var }}) %>%
    mutate(
      percent = percent(n / sum(n), accuracy = 0.1)
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(n)) %>%
    gt() %>%
    tab_header(
      title = var_label
    ) %>%
    fmt_missing(
      columns = everything(),
      missing_text = "NA"
    )
}

#===========================================================
# Data: remove NA only for categories where needed
#===========================================================

df_subclass <- df %>% filter(!is.na(cancer_subclass) & cancer_subclass != "")

#===========================================================
# PANEL TABLES
#===========================================================

# 1. Sex
table_sex <- make_table(df, sex, "Sex Distribution")

# 2. Race
table_race <- make_table(df, race, "Race Distribution")

# 3. Ethnicity
table_ethnicity <- make_table(df, ethnicity, "Ethnicity Distribution")

# 4. Cancer Type
table_cancer_type <- make_table(df, cancer_type, "Cancer Type")

# 5. Cancer Subclass (only those with defined subclasses)
table_cancer_subclass <- make_table(df_subclass, cancer_subclass, "Cancer Subclass")


#===========================================================
# OPTIONAL: Print all tables in sequence
#===========================================================

table_sex
table_race
table_ethnicity
table_cancer_type
table_cancer_subclass
```
#Printing tables in one pdf
```{r}
tbl_sex <- df %>%
  select(sex) %>%
  tbl_summary(statistic = all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)", missing = "no")

tbl_race <- df %>%
  select(race) %>%
  tbl_summary(statistic = all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)", missing = "no")

tbl_ethnicity <- df %>%
  select(ethnicity) %>%
  tbl_summary(statistic = all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)", missing = "no")

tbl_cancer_type <- df %>%
  select(cancer_type) %>%
  tbl_summary(statistic = all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)", missing = "no")


tbl_cancer_subtype <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(cancer_subclass) & cancer_subclass != "") %>%
  select(cancer_type, cancer_subclass) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = cancer_type,
    statistic = all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)",
    missing = "no"
  ) %>%
  modify_header(label = "**Cancer Subtype**") %>%
  modify_spanning_header(
    all_stat_cols() ~ "**N (% within cancer type)**"
  )


table_all <- tbl_stack(
  tbls = list(
    tbl_sex,
    tbl_race,
    tbl_ethnicity,
    tbl_cancer_type,
    tbl_cancer_subtype
  ),
  group_header = c(
    "Sex",
    "Race",
    "Ethnicity",
    "Cancer Type",
    "Cancer Subtype (Within Cancer Type)"
  )
)
table_all_gt <- table_all %>%
  as_gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Table 1. Cohort Demographic and Clinical Characteristics",
    subtitle = paste0("Total cohort size: N = ", nrow(df))
  )

gtsave(table_all_gt, "out/1b/table1_cohort_demographics.pdf")
```
```{r}
# ==============================
# Load Packages
# ==============================
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(RColorBrewer)
#library(ggpubr)
library(stringr)
#library(ggforce)

# ==============================
# 1️⃣ Prepare Data
# ==============================

# Cancer types
cancer_type_df <- df %>%
  count(cancer_type) %>%
  mutate(p = n / sum(n))

# Top 5 subtypes per cancer (Main Figure)
subtype_df_top <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(cancer_subclass) & cancer_subclass != "") %>%
  count(cancer_type, cancer_subclass) %>%
  group_by(cancer_type) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = n, n = 5) %>%
  mutate(p = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(cancer_subclass = fct_reorder(cancer_subclass, p))

# Extended Data: All subtypes, collapse rare (<1%) into "Other"
subtype_df_all <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(cancer_subclass) & cancer_subclass != "") %>%
  count(cancer_type, cancer_subclass) %>%
  group_by(cancer_type) %>%
  mutate(
    p = n / sum(n),
    cancer_subclass = ifelse(p < 0.01, "Other", as.character(cancer_subclass))
  ) %>%
  group_by(cancer_type, cancer_subclass) %>%
  summarise(
    n = sum(n),
    p = sum(p),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(cancer_type, desc(p)) %>%
  mutate(
    cancer_subclass = factor(cancer_subclass, levels = unique(cancer_subclass)),
    cancer_subclass_wrapped = str_wrap(cancer_subclass, width = 10)
  )

# Order cancer types by cohort prevalence
cancer_type_order <- df %>%
  count(cancer_type) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  pull(cancer_type)

subtype_df_all$cancer_type <- factor(subtype_df_all$cancer_type, levels = cancer_type_order)
subtype_df_top$cancer_type <- factor(subtype_df_top$cancer_type, levels = cancer_type_order)
cancer_type_df$cancer_type <- factor(cancer_type_df$cancer_type, levels = cancer_type_order)

# ==============================
# 2️⃣ Main Figure Panels
# ==============================

# Panel A: Cancer Types Dot Plot
p_cancer_type <- ggplot(cancer_type_df, aes(x = p, y = cancer_type)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#2c7bb6") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(x = "% of cohort", y = NULL, title = "Cancer Type Distribution") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

# Panel B: Top 5 Subtypes per Cancer (Faceted Dot Plot)
p_subtype_top <- ggplot(subtype_df_top, aes(x = p, y = cancer_subclass)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, color = "#fc8d59") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  facet_wrap(~ cancer_type, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "% within cancer type", y = NULL, title = "Top 5 Subtypes per Cancer Type") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

# Arrange main figure (two-column layout)
main_figure <- ggarrange(
  p_cancer_type, p_subtype_top,
  ncol = 2,
  labels = c("A", "B"),
  widths = c(1, 1.5)
)

# Save main figure
ggsave("out/1b/main_figure_cancer_distribution.pdf", main_figure, width = 12, height = 6)
ggsave("out/1b/main_figure_cancer_distribution.tiff", main_figure, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 600)

# ==============================
# 3️⃣ Extended Data Heatmap
# ==============================

# Optional subtle grouping background by cancer type
subtype_positions <- subtype_df_all %>%
  group_by(cancer_type) %>%
  summarise(
    x_min = min(as.numeric(cancer_subclass_wrapped)) - 0.5,
    x_max = max(as.numeric(cancer_subclass_wrapped)) + 0.5,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p_subtype_heatmap <- ggplot(subtype_df_all, aes(x = cancer_subclass_wrapped, y = cancer_type, fill = p)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_rect(data = subtype_positions,
            aes(xmin = x_min, xmax = x_max, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            fill = "grey95", alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = brewer.pal(9, "YlGnBu"),
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(subtype_df_all$p))
  ) +
  labs(x = "Cancer Subtype", y = "Cancer Type", fill = "% within cancer type",
       title = "Cancer Subtype Distribution (Extended Data)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 8) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

# Save Extended Data figure
ggsave("out/1b/extended_data_subtype_heatmap.pdf", p_subtype_heatmap, width = 12, height = 8)
ggsave("out/1b/extended_data_subtype_heatmap.tiff", p_subtype_heatmap, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 600)
```
#New table as of 1/8/26
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(gtsummary)
library(gt)
library(forcats)

#-------------------------------------------------
# Create age groups (adjust cutpoints if needed)
#-------------------------------------------------
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    age_years = age_at_diagnosis / 365.25,
    age_group = case_when(
      age_years < 1   ~ "<1",
      age_years >= 1  & age_years < 5   ~ "1–4",
      age_years >= 5  & age_years < 10  ~ "5–9",
      age_years >= 10 & age_years < 15  ~ "10–14",
      age_years >= 15 & age_years < 19  ~ "15–18",
      age_years >= 19 & age_years < 40  ~ "19–40",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    age_group = factor(
      age_group,
      levels = c("<1", "1–4", "5–9", "10–14", "15–18", "19–40"),
      ordered = TRUE
    )
  )

#-------------------------------------------------
# Group rare race categories (<5 → Other)
#-------------------------------------------------
race_counts <- df %>%
  count(race)

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    race_clean = case_when(
      race == "American Indian Or Alaska Native; White" ~ "American Indian Or Alaska Native",
      race == "Asian; White" ~ "Asian",
      race == "Black Or African American; White" ~ "Black Or African American",
      TRUE ~ race
    )
  ) %>%
  add_count(race_clean) %>%
  mutate(
    race_collapsed = if_else(n < 5, "Other", race_clean),
    race_collapsed = fct_infreq(race_collapsed),
    race_collapsed = fct_relevel(race_collapsed, "Other", after = Inf) #order Other last
  ) %>%
  select(-n) %>%
  mutate(
    cancer_type = fct_infreq(cancer_type)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sex = as.factor(sex),
    race_collapsed = as.factor(race_collapsed),
    ethnicity = as.factor(ethnicity)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sex = fct_na_value_to_level(sex, "Not Reported"),
    race_collapsed = fct_na_value_to_level(race_collapsed, "Not Reported"),
    ethnicity = fct_na_value_to_level(ethnicity, "Not Reported")
  )

```
#create main table
```{r}
table1_main <- df %>%
  select(
    sex,
    age_group,
    race_collapsed,
    ethnicity#,
    #cancer_type
  ) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    statistic = all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)",
    missing = "no",
    label = list(
      sex ~ "Sex",
      age_group ~ "Age at Diagnosis (years)",
      race_collapsed ~ "Race",
      ethnicity ~ "Ethnicity"#,
      #cancer_type ~ "Cancer Type"
    )
  ) %>%
  modify_header(label = "") %>%
  #modify_spanning_header(
  #  all_stat_cols() ~ "**N (% of cohort)**"
  #) %>%
  #modify_caption(
  #  "**Table 1. Cohort Demographic and Clinical Characteristics**"
  #) %>%
  bold_labels()
```
#convert to gt
```{r}
table1_main_gt <- table1_main %>%
  as_gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = md("**Table 1. Cohort Demographic and Clinical Characteristics**")#,
    #subtitle = paste0("Total cohort size: N = ", nrow(df))
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    md("Values are shown as *N* (%). Race categories with fewer than 5 individuals were aggregated into 'Other'.")
  ) %>%
  opt_row_striping() %>%
  tab_options(
    table.font.size = px(11),
    data_row.padding = px(3)
  )
```
#save to table
```{r}
gtsave(table1_main_gt, "out/1b/Table1_Main_Cohort_Characteristics.pdf")
gtsave(table1_main_gt, "out/1b/Table1_Main_Cohort_Characteristics.html")
```
